title: Inoue transformation buffer
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"c9nb0","text":"The Inoue method of transformation is a chemical transformation method of prokaryotes (bacteria), which add significant improvements to methods such as the classic Calcium chloride transformation method.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Add 80 mL distilled water to a 100 mL tube

Measure and add 10.89 g Manganese chloride, 2.2 g Calcium chloride, 3.0 g PIPES and 18.65 g Potassium chloride Materials: Manganese(II) chloride tetrahydrate Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #M3634 (CAS: 13446-34-9) Calcium chloride dihydrate Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #C3881 PIPES Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #P1851 (CAS: 5625-37-6) Potassium chloride Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #P3911 (CAS: 7447-40-7)
Filter sterilize with a 0.2 µm pore-size filter and store frozen at -20 °C
creator: Andreas Sagen
